data.content:
Now I have the full picture. Let me create the `Directory.Packages.props` and update all project files to remove versions.

**Version conflicts resolved (highest wins):**
- `System.Text.Json`: 10.0.1 (was 8.0.4 in Worker)
- `Azure.Identity`: 1.13.2 (was 1.11.4 in Shared)

arguments.file_text:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Azure.Identity" Version="1.13.2" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="10.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Now I have all the information I need. Let me identify the packages and their versions across all projects:

- System.Text.Json: 10.0.1 (Api), 10.0.1 (Shared), 8.0.4 (Worker), 10.0.1 (Tests) → highest: 10.0.1
- Azure.Identity: 1.13.2 (Api), 1.11.4 (Shared) → highest: 1.13.2
- Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting: 8.0.1 (Worker) → 8.0.1


- OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting: 1.15.0 (Worker) → 1.15.0
- xunit: 2.9.3 (Tests) → 2.9.3

Now I need to create a Directory.Packages.props file with centralized version management enabled, add all the package versions there, and then remove the Version attributes from each project's PackageReference elements. Let me first check if this file already exists.

arguments.file_text:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Azure.Identity" Version="1.13.2" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="10.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

Done. The solution now uses Central Package Management:

- **Created** `Directory.Packages.props` with `ManagePackageVersionsCentrally` enabled and all 5 packages centralized
- **Removed** `Version` attributes from all `PackageReference` entries in the 4 `.csproj` files
- **Resolved conflicts** by picking the highest version:

  - `Azure.Identity` → 1.13.2 (Shared had 1.11.4)
